ranked_movies.title: Airplane!
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a quintessential comedy that successfully parodies its genre (disaster movies). Multiple users across threads praised it for its humor and cultural impact, with one noting it's a prime example of a parody more famous than the original.

ranked_movies.title: The Naked Gun
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for its slapstick style and as a benchmark for comedy franchises. Several comments mentioned it in the context of successful comedy series and its recent reboot.

ranked_movies.title: Hot Fuzz
ranked_movies.reason: Part of the Cornetto Trilogy, which was repeatedly mentioned as a strong contender. Users highlighted its blend of action and comedy, and its status as a modern classic.

ranked_movies.title: Shaun of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Another cornerstone of the Cornetto Trilogy, widely praised for its humor and horror elements. It was directly recommended in several threads as a top-tier comedy.

ranked_movies.title: Barbie
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a recent example of a successful comedy on a big budget. One user noted it as evidence that comedies can still be made and be successful, contrary to the idea that the genre is dead.

ranked_movies.title: Bridesmaids
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a successful female-led comedy that had cultural impact. It was mentioned in discussions about the state of comedy movies.

ranked_movies.title: Superbad
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a defining comedy of its generation. Users brought it up as a standard for modern comedies.

ranked_movies.title: Zoolander
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced as an example of a comedy that was widely quoted and loved, part of the discussion on what made comedies successful in the past.

ranked_movies.title: Deadpool
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a recent successful comedy-action hybrid. Its success shows the appetite for humor in blockbuster films.

ranked_movies.title: The Lost City
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as a recent action-comedy that performed well, showing the genre's viability. One user pointed to it as an example of the kind of movie still being made.

ranked_movies.title: What We Do in the Shadows
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its unique take on comedy-horror. It was mentioned in discussions about niche genres that still produce quality content.

ranked_movies.title: Palm Springs
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a prime example of a comedy that went straight to streaming, illustrating the shift in how such movies are distributed. It was discussed as a movie that would have been a hit in theaters in another era.

ranked_movies.title: Bottoms
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as a recent comedy that was critically acclaimed. It was brought up as evidence that comedies are still being made, even if they are not all theatrical releases.

ranked_movies.title: The Nice Guys
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as an example of a critically acclaimed comedy that did not find a huge audience but is highly regarded by those who saw it.

ranked_movies.title: Scott Pilgrim vs. The World
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a cult classic that has gained appreciation over time. It was noted for its unique style and humor.

ranked_movies.title: 21 Jump Street
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up as a successful comedy-action reboot that managed to be both funny and successful at the box office.

ranked_movies.title: The Other Guys
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as an example of a comedy that worked despite not being a typical genre piece. It was noted for its cast and writing.

ranked_movies.title: The Heat
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the context of buddy-cop comedies that have worked. It was part of the discussion on female-led comedies.

ranked_movies.title: Game Night
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a well-received comedy-thriller. It was noted for its pacing and humor.

ranked_movies.title: Knives Out
ranked_movies.reason: While more of a mystery, its humor was noted. It was mentioned as an example of a movie that successfully blended genres.
